data.title: Major Warner Bros. stockholder says Paramount's ameded bid 'necessary, but not sufficient'
data.description:
Paramount Skydance's (PSKY) $30 per share bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), which now includes a personal equity financing guarantee of $40.4B from Larry Ellison, is still not good enough for Harris Associates, the fifth-largest shareholder with a nearly 4% stake in the HBO owner.

"The changes in Paramount’s new offer were necessary, but not sufficient," Alex Fitch, the portfolio manager and director of U.S. research at the investing firm, told Reuters in an email. "We see the two deals as a toss-up, and there is a cost to changing paths. If Paramount is serious about winning, they’re going to need to provide a greater incentive."

The investor had said last week that they would welcome an increased bid from the David Ellison-led media company.

Along with the Oracle founder's personal guarantee, Paramount on Monday raised its termination fee and has agreed not to revoke the Ellison family trust or transfer its assets while the transaction is pending but did not budge from the $30 per share offer.

The company also extended the tender offer deadline for Warner Bros. shareholders to January 21, from January 8. As of December 19, only about 400,000 WBD shares have been tendered, and Warner Bros. has 2,479,486,688 shares of common stock outstanding, as per its latest filing. A source told Bloomberg last week that bidders have been using a 2.59B share total.

Netflix (NFLX) is currently the front-runner to buy the streaming and studio assets of Warner Bros. in a $72B deal, excluding debt. The streaming giant has reworked a portion of the $59B bridge financing, which will be used for the deal, by securing a $5B credit line and two $10B delayed-draw loans, leaving $34B for syndication.

data.title: TrueCar goes private after shareholders approve a buyout
data.description:
Shareholders with TrueCar (TRUE) approved the deal to be taken private by Fair Holdings. The all-cash merger values the company at $2.55 per share, or about $227 million. The deal is structured such that a Fair Holdings merger subsidiary will combine with TrueCar (TRUE), which will then become a wholly owned subsidiary of Fair Holdings and be delisted from Nasdaq.

Fair Holdings is a founder-led investor vehicle controlled by TrueCar (TRUE) original founder Scott Painter, who is expected to return as CEO of TrueCar after the transaction closes, marking a comeback roughly a decade after his 2015 departure.

Large shareholders such as AutoNation's (AN) affiliate and Caledonia supported the buyout.

"Approval of the transaction by stockholders is a pivotal moment for TrueCar," stated TrueCar (RUE) CEO Jantoon Reigersman. "This transaction is a win-win for our Company, our investors, our affinity partner network, certified dealers, and car buyers nationwide," he added.

TrueCar (TRUE) traded as high as $25 shortly following its IPO in 2014, but its business model has struggled amid the evolving used car environment.

data.title: Stock futures turn marginally red as traders parse GDP data, looks towards Santa rally
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures gave up their slight gains to turn marginally lower on Tuesday, as investors parsed delayed economic growth data. 

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) slipped -0.20%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) fell -0.07%, and Dow futures (INDU) edged lower by -0.04%.

According to the Bureau of Economic Analysis, U.S. Q3 GDP annualized growth of +4.3% blew past  the consensus figure of +3.2%. However, delving past the topline beat, the report showed some weakness.

"The main issue in the Q3 data is the strong performance of household consumption against weak real incomes. Ex-transfers, real income is flat over the last two quarters while consumption is expanding 3%. Either income is being underreported or we're seeing an unsustainable drawdown in savings," Renaissance Macro Research said on X.

The focus this week is also on the Santa Claus rally. After a topsy-turvy December, markets have reapproached record levels.

"The official Santa Claus Rally period starts on Wednesday. Note, we didn't see Santa last year and clearly had a rough Q1. It is when Santa doesn't show up that we should be worried," Ryan Detrick, chief market strategist at Carson Group, said on X on Monday.

The Santa Claus rally refers to a historical trend that has seen the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) post gains during the last five trading days of the year and the first two of the next year. See the chart Detrick shared below:

U.S. Treasury yields reversed course after the GDP data, rising across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed to 4.18%. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose to 3.53%, while the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) increased to 4.84%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Albemarle (ALB) +2.95%, Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) +2.54%, and Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) +1.56%. Decliners included Henry Schein (HSIC) -6.53%, Sandisk (SNDK) -1.97%, and Robinhood (HOOD) -1.79%.

data.title: Nvidia, AMD could see upside from China, Raymond James says
data.description:
• In light of recent events, investment firm Raymond James believes Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) could see moderate upside from China.
  • “Several moving pieces remain, making it difficult to assess the exact outcome, but in an opportunistic scenario, we estimate AMD could see ~$500-$800M of revenue upside and ~$0.10-0.20 of non-GAAP EPS upside, and NVDA could see ~$7-$12.5B of revenue upside, which translates to ~$0.15-0.30 of non-GAAP EPS upside in 2026,” analyst Simon Leopold wrote in a note to clients. Leopold reiterated his Strong Buy rating on Nvidia and an Outperform rating on AMD.
  • Earlier this month, President Trump said he had approved the sale of Nvidia's H200 GPUs to China. On Monday, media reports suggested that Alibaba (BABA) was considering purchasing between 40,000 and 50,000 of AMD's MI308 AI accelerators.
  • Leopold said the potential order from Alibaba could be worth $675M in sales, albeit over several quarters. 
  • Nvidia and AMD shares were down around 0.7% in premarket trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Cooper Cos. appoints Rosebrough to board, enters agreement with Browning West
data.description:
Cooper Cos. (COO)  announced that it appointed Walter Rosebrough Jr.  as an independent director to its board, effective Jan. 3. 

In connection with his appointment, the company entered into a cooperation agreement with Browning West, Cooper said in a  statement on Tuesday. Rosebrough will join the board's corporate governance and nominating committee. The board also agreed that by the end of next year it will give "due and serious consideration" to appointing Rosebrough chair of the board. 

The board also agreed to identify and appoint a new independent director with medical technology experience, with the mutual agreement of Browning West.

The board news comes after Cooper Cos. (COO) earlier this month said it started a  strategic review of its businesses and named a new board chair amid pressure from activist investor Jana Partners. The company named Colleen Jay as chairman to succeed Robert Weiss, effective Jan. 2.

Rosebrough currently serves as CEO Emeritus and senior advisor of Steris plc (STE).  His career also includes nearly two decades at Hill-Rom Holdings, Inc., where he held senior executive positions.

data.title: RTX's Raytheon wins $1.7 billion contract to deliver four Patriot fire units to Spain
data.description:
• Raytheon, a business of RTX Corp. (RTX), has been awarded a $1.7 billion contract to provide Spain with four Patriot air and missile defense systems, marking the country’s largest-ever order for the system, the company said Tuesday.
  • The foreign military sales contract includes radars, launchers, command and control stations, and training equipment.
  • The contract comes as Germany, the Netherlands, and Romania have placed orders for additional Patriot systems in 2025.

data.title: Cassava Sciences to pay $31.25M to settle securities class action suit
data.description:
• Cassava Sciences (SAVA) announced on Tuesday that it agreed to pay $31.25M to settle a class action lawsuit pending in a Texas District Court over the company’s alleged violations of the federal securities laws.
  • The case filed in the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas, Austin Division, in 2021 relied on allegations contained in Citizen Petitions submitted to the FDA regarding Cassava’s (SAVA) lead asset, simufilam.
  • In November 2024, the company suffered a late-stage trial setback for the once high-flying oral therapy targeted at Alzheimer's disease.
  • “The settlement is not an admission of fault or wrongdoing by the Company,” Cassava (SAVA) said, adding that it has recorded a $31.25M provision in Q2 2025 to recognize a potential loss contingency related to the case.

data.title: Canada’s monthly GDP falls 0.3% in October after September gain
data.description: • The monthly GDP MoM in Canada decreased to -0.30 percent in October from 0.20 percent in September of 2025.

data.title: Kroger approves additional $2B share buyback authorization
data.description:
• Kroger’s (KR) board approved an incremental $2B share repurchase authorization, supplementing the $7.5B program announced in December 2024.
  • Following the new approval, Kroger has ~$2.9B remaining under its total share repurchase authorizations as of December 23, 2025.
  • Share repurchases are expected to be funded through operating cash flow and existing liquidity, while maintaining Kroger’s investment-grade credit rating.

data.title: U.S. Gold raises ~$31.2M in private placement; shares down
data.description:
• U.S. Gold (USAU) closed a private placement issuing 1.92M common shares at $16.25/share and warrants for 961,077 shares, generating ~$31.2M in gross proceeds.
  • The warrants are immediately exercisable at $23/share and expire two years from issuance.
  • The offering price represented an ~4% discount to the December 15, 2025 closing price of $16.91.
  • New institutional shareholders include Franklin Templeton Investments, Mackenzie Investments, and Libra Advisors.
  • Proceeds will be used for initial development of the CK Gold Project, potential land acquisitions, exploration activities, and general working capital.
  • USAU shares down 8% premarket on Tuesday.
